Agent Instructions for rtfreporter

Source: AGENTS.md

⚠️ Mandatory first step

Before modifying any code, test, or documentation, always read the spec files first.

vignettes/articles/internal-design.qmd   ← Internal S3 class design
vignettes/articles/external-api.qmd      ← Public S3 API contracts
specs/release_guidelines.md              ← Release and packaging rules

The spec documents are the single source of truth. All R code, tests, and vignettes must conform to the specs. If a spec and code disagree, the spec wins unless the user explicitly asks to change the spec first.

Workflow rule

  1. Read relevant spec sections before writing any code.
  2. Update specs first when a design decision changes (API shape, behavior, defaults, data structures).
  3. Then update in this order:
    • R implementation (r/)
    • R help (man/, roxygen comments in r/)
    • R vignettes (vignettes/)
    • R tests (tests/)
  4. Never introduce an API pattern in code that is not documented in the specs.

Repository structure

This repository is the R package (DESCRIPTION is at the root).

rtfreporter/                        ← repo root = R package root (CRAN-ready layout)
├── AGENTS.md                       ← This file (read first)
├── DESCRIPTION                     ← R package manifest
├── NAMESPACE                       ← Generated by roxygen2
├── LICENSE
├── README.md
├── CHANGELOG.md
├── rtfreporter.Rproj
│
├── r/                              ← R source files (all S3)
│   ├── rtfreport.R                 ← internal rtfreport S3, rtf_header(), rtf_footer()
│   ├── rtf_border.R                ← Border class hierarchy (S3)
│   ├── rtf_table_style.R           ← Shared table style record (S3)
│   ├── generate_rtfreport.R        ← RTF renderer + all render helpers
│   ├── pipe-composition.R          ← S3 pipe API (rtf_document, rtf_section, etc.)
│   ├── rtftable.R                  ← internal .new_rtftable() (returns class "rtftable")
│   ├── rtfplot.R                   ← internal .new_rtfplot() (returns class "rtfplot")
│   ├── wrappers.R                  ← Public S3 constructors: rtftable(), rtfplot()
│   ├── blank_rows.R                ← blank_rows_by_change(), blank_rows_by_rule()
│   ├── assemble_rtf.R              ← assemble_rtf()
│   └── text_width.R                ← text_width_in(), auto_col_widths()
│
├── man/                            ← R help files (*.Rd) — regenerate with roxygen2
│
├── tests/                          ← R tests
│   ├── test-rtf-generation.R       ← End-to-end RTF generation test (must pass)
│   └── test-pipe-api.R             ← Pipe API unit tests (must pass)
│
├── vignettes/
│   ├── rtfreporter-quickstart.Rmd  ← User-facing quickstart
│   ├── rtfreporter-pipes.Rmd       ← Pipe API guide
│   └── articles/                   ← Design docs (pkgdown articles)
│       ├── internal-design.qmd     ← Internal S3 class design
│       └── external-api.qmd        ← Public S3 API spec
│
├── inst/
│   ├── doc/                        ← Pre-built vignette HTML (git-tracked)
│   └── resources/
│       ├── rtf_commands.R          ← RTF control word templates
│       └── rtfreporter_defaults.R  ← Configurable defaults
│
└── specs/
    └── release_guidelines.md       ← Release and packaging rules

Key API rules (summary — see specs for full detail)

Public API: S3 pipe pattern

The primary user-facing API uses the pipe (%>%) pattern:

report <- rtf_document() %>%
  rtf_section(page = 1, secinfo = list(header = hdr, footer = ftr)) %>%
  rtf_tables(list(rtftable(df1), rtftable(df2))) %>%
  generate_rtfreport("output.rtf", overwrite = TRUE)

Internal class naming (all S3 since v0.0.19)

Internal data classes are plain S3 tagged lists built by package-internal constructors (prefixed with .new_*):

  • rtfreport — document object (internal .new_rtfreport())
  • rtftable — table content (public rtftable() → .new_rtftable())
  • rtfplot — figure content (public rtfplot() → .new_rtfplot())
  • rtf_page, rtf_sect — records living inside rtfreport

rtfreport is internal-only; the public path is to build a rtf_document via the pipe API and let generate_rtfreport() convert it internally via .pipe_doc_to_rtfreport(). Use the .rtfreport_add_page() / .rtfreport_add_section() / .rtfreport_validate() functional helpers when manipulating an rtfreport from inside the package — each returns a new copy.

R6 is no longer used anywhere; Imports: R6 was removed in v0.0.19.

Border class hierarchy

# Single edge
s <- rtf_border_side(style = "single", width = 15L, color = NULL)

# Four edges
b <- rtf_border(top = s, bottom = NULL, left = NULL, right = NULL)

# Convenience
rtf_border_none()
rtf_border_top(style, width, color)
rtf_border_bottom(style, width, color)
rtf_border_box(style, width, color)

# Per-zone table border
tb <- rtf_table_border(header=..., spanning=..., body=..., first_row=..., last_row=...)
rtf_border_tfl()   # Clinical TFL preset

Header/Footer constructors

hdr <- rtf_header(
  rows = list(
    c(l = "Protocol: RTF-101", r = "Company"),
    c(l = "Table 14.1.1",     r = "Page {AUTO_PAGE} of {AUTO_TOTAL_PAGES}")
  )
)
ftr <- rtf_footer(c(l = "Confidential"))

Page tokens

Token Behavior
{AUTO_PAGE} \chpgn — dynamic page number (recommended)
{AUTO_TOTAL_PAGES} RTF NUMPAGES field — dynamic total (recommended)
{PAGE} Alias for {AUTO_PAGE}
{TOTAL_PAGES} Static total page count at render time

Recommended: "Page {AUTO_PAGE} of {AUTO_TOTAL_PAGES}".

Running tests

cd C:\Yrepo\rtfreporter
Rscript tests/test-rtf-generation.R
Rscript tests/test-pipe-api.R

All tests must pass before any commit to main.

Vignette rendering (pre-built HTML)

cd C:\Yrepo\rtfreporter

# 1. Build vignettes
Rscript -e "devtools::build_vignettes()"

# 2. Copy to inst/doc/
Copy-Item "doc\*" "inst\doc\" -Recurse -Force

# 3. Verify
Rscript -e "devtools::install(); vignette(package='rtfreporter')"

Articles (in vignettes/articles/) are Quarto documents rendered separately:

cd C:\Yrepo\rtfreporter\vignettes\articles
quarto render internal-design.qmd
quarto render external-api.qmd

On this page
